prd.user_story: As a platform engineer onboarding new service teams to Kubernetes, I want to identify the canonical patterns for deploying new services and understand how they are defined and documented across the Kubernetes ecosystem repos.
constraints: Identify the authoritative Deployment struct definition in the Kubernetes API types repo.
Find the canonical Go code example showing how to create a Deployment using the client library.
Locate the README or documentation file that explains the deployment workflow.
prd.success_definition: Agent identifies: (1) sg-evals/kubernetes-api apps/v1/types.go (Deployment struct), (2) sg-evals/kubernetes-client-go examples/create-update-delete-deployment/main.go (deployment creation pattern), and (3) sg-evals/kubernetes-client-go examples/create-update-delete-deployment/README.md (workflow documentation).
prd.seed_prompt: Find the canonical patterns for deploying new services in Kubernetes and how they are documented across the kubernetes-api (type definitions), kubernetes-client-go (code examples), and etcd (storage documentation) repos.

deepsearch_variant.variant_prompt: Use Deep Search to find and synthesize canonical deployment patterns across kubernetes-api, kubernetes-client-go, and etcd repositories.
deepsearch_variant.synthesis_focus: service deployment pattern discovery across Kubernetes ecosystem
